message:
fix(scanner): replace @copilot dispatch with parallel worktree agents (#164)

* fix(scanner): replace @copilot dispatch with parallel worktree agents

Scanner was interpreting "dispatch fix agents" as "post @copilot fix
comments on GitHub issues" — 64+ useless comments in one day. The kick
message now explicitly prescribes the worktree workflow (create worktree,
fix, commit, push, PR) and tells scanner to dispatch 4-6 sub-agents in
parallel rather than single-threading fixes.

Also blocks gh search issues/prs alongside the existing gh issue/pr list
prohibition.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: remove Task/Agent tool references from scanner kick message

Scanner runs on Copilot CLI which has no Task or Agent tool. Simplified
the workflow to sequential worktree steps that any CLI can execute.
Also made merge instruction explicit: gh pr merge --squash from the
merge-ready list.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -745,8 +745,9 @@ fi
 SCANNER_MSG="[agent:scanner] [KICK] git pull /tmp/hive. ${_SCANNER_POLICY_INSTR}
 YOUR WORK LIST (pre-filtered — hold/ADOPTERS/drafts excluded, classified):
 ${_WORK_LIST}${_CLUSTER_SECTION}${_MERGE_INLINE}
-⛔ NEVER run gh issue list or gh pr list — the work list above is your ONLY source. You may use gh issue view, gh pr view, gh pr merge, gh pr create on individual items.
-Use the model_recommendation for each issue (S=haiku, M=sonnet, C=opus). Bundle clustered issues into 1 agent per cluster. Skip issues with lane!=scanner. Dispatch fix agents for 4-6 oldest issues across ALL repos, merge only from the merge-ready list. Do NOT stand by — if issues exist, work them. NEVER run vitest, npm test, npm run build, tsc, or any test/build locally. Beads: ~/scanner-beads"
+⛔ NEVER run gh issue list, gh pr list, gh search issues, or gh search prs — the work list above is your ONLY source. You may use gh issue view, gh pr view, gh pr merge, gh pr create on individual items.
+⛔ NEVER post @copilot or @claude comments on issues. NEVER use gh issue comment to dispatch work. NEVER assign copilot-swe-agent[bot]. Posting @copilot comments does nothing and wastes cycles.
+WORKFLOW for each issue: 1) git worktree add /tmp/fix-NNNN -b fix/NNNN from the repo checkout, 2) cd into worktree, read the issue with gh issue view, make the fix, 3) git add + git commit -s, 4) git push origin fix/NNNN, 5) gh pr create --body 'Fixes #NNNN'. Work 4-6 issues per kick. Use the model_recommendation for each issue (S=haiku, M=sonnet, C=opus). Bundle clustered issues into 1 worktree per cluster. Skip issues with lane!=scanner. Merge PRs from the merge-ready list using gh pr merge --squash. Do NOT stand by — if issues exist, work them. NEVER run vitest, npm test, npm run build, tsc, or any test/build locally. Beads: ~/scanner-beads"
 
 # Build live health preamble for reviewer — tells it exactly what's red RIGHT NOW
 _rh_json=$(/tmp/hive/dashboard/health-check.sh 2>/dev/null || echo '{}')
